results.title: Commitment issues? Analysing the effect of preference deviation and social embeddedness on member commitment to worker cooperatives in the gig economy
results.abstract: <jats:p>As enterprises that are owned and governed by workers themselves for their mutual benefit, worker cooperatives are currently re-emerging as a promising antidote against precarity and economic dependence in the gig economy. results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Employment law recognised the value of whistleblowing with the enactment of the Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998, protecting a ‘worker’ against dismissal and victimisation. Whistleblowers are particularly vulnerable in the gig economy as they may fall outside the statutory definition of ‘worker’ for the purposes of the whistleblowing legislation. results.title: Analytical Study of Interest Rate Spread on Private Consumption, Savings and Investment in Nigeria
results.abstract: <jats:p>The paper examined how interest rate spreads affected private consumption, investments, and savings in Nigeria. The Ordinary Lease Square (OLS) was used. Descriptive statistics, the short-run ECM mode were all investigated in this study. According to the study, interest rate spread significantly and favorably affects savings, investments, and private consumption.
